data.title: Taiwan May Sustain Healthy Exports After Front-Loading Demand Wanes — Market Talk
data.description: Taiwan's export performance could remain healthy even after front-loading demand wanes, ANZ's Bansi Madhavani says in a note. Although the record run in Taiwanese exports this year was driven partly by front-loading activity ahead of U.S. tariffs, underlying demand for semiconductor products is central to the island's export demand, the economist says. Madhavani expects Taiwan to reach some form of a trade framework or an interim arrangement with the U.S. ahead of the Aug. 1 deadline, with the original 32% reciprocal tariff negotiated down. "The key watchpoint of such an arrangement will be whether semiconductors continue to remain exempt from tariffs." (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: Tesla's New Model Y L May Boost China Sales Only Marginally — Market Talk
data.description: Tesla's new six-seat Model Y L may only boost the carmaker's China sales marginally, Daiwa analysts write in a note. The company said on its Weibo account that it will release a new six-seater Model Y L this fall, but Daiwa thinks the overall dimensions of the model is smaller than many current six-seater cars in the market. Tesla is effectively fitting a six-seat configuration into a chassis traditionally designed for five seats, it says. Although that may result in compromised passenger comfort, brand loyalty could drive consumer willingness to accept this, Daiwa adds. That said, reduced legroom and relatively high pricing means the new model could only contribute to a modest recovery to Tesla's sales in China, Daiwa says. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

data.title: Kangmei Pharmaceutical's Unit to Settle 44 Million Yuan in Debt
data.description:
Kangmei Pharmaceutical's unit, Chengdu Kangmei Pharmaceutical Production, agreed to settle 43.5 million yuan of debt to Guangzhou Pharmaceutical Group, according to a Shanghai bourse filing on Friday.

After receiving the full payment, Guangzhou Pharma will withdraw the suit it filed at the Guangdong High People's Court against the pharmaceutical company in 2023.

Chengdu Kangmei is required to pay the settlement within 30 days.

Guangzhou Pharmaceutical is a partner of Guangdong Shennong Enterprise Management, Kangmei Pharma's controlling shareholder.

data.title: Mineral Commodities Receives Partial Payments in Norway Project Sale
data.description:
Mineral Commodities received a non-refundable payment of 1 million pounds sterling from Norge Mineraler for the sale of its Skaland Graphite project in Norway, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

Norge will make available up to 410,000 pounds sterling for costs, which will not be applied against the purchase price.

The company expects to complete the deal by Oct. 31, the filing added.

data.title: Leoch International: Significant Cost Increase Due to U.S. Tariffs Weighed on Results
Leoch International Expects 1H Revenue to Increase 10%-20% on Year
Leoch International Expects 1H Net Profit to Fall 60%-80% on Year
United Cooperative Assurance Appoints Financial Advisor for Potential Merger with Arabia Insurance Cooperative
data.description:
United Cooperative Assurance (SASE:8190) appointed Estidamah Capital (SASE:1175) as its financial adviser for the proposed merger with Arabia Insurance Cooperative (SASE:8160), according to a Thursday release.

The Saudi-listed insurers signed a nonbinding memorandum of understanding on June 19 to assess the potential merger and negotiate the terms.

data.title: American Rare Earths Unit to Conduct Optimisation Tests at US Project
data.description:
American Rare Earths' US unit, Wyoming Rare, will undertake additional mineral processing optimization tests at the Cowboy State Mine, part of the Halleck Creek Rare Earths Project in the US, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The tests will evaluate multiple milling methods and optimise the beneficiation circuit, the filing said.

The company also plans to begin test mining in the near term to extract feedstock, the filing added.

Shares of the company fell past 1% in recent Friday trade.

data.title: Gaush Meditech Unit Gains Guangdong Nod for Contact Lens; Shares Rise 4%
data.description:
Gaush Meditech said its subsidiary Gaush Tech has received a medical device registration certificate from the Guangdong Medical Products Administration for its corneal contact lens, according to a Thursday Hong Kong bourse filing.

Shares of the company were up about 4% in Friday morning trade.

The approved lens assists in the scanning of eye tissues when used with the appropriate diagnostic equipment.

The product is designed to help diagnose eye diseases, according to the filing.

data.title: EMEA Morning Briefing: Upbeat Corporate Earnings Boost Optimism
data.description:
MARKET WRAPS

Watch For:

No major economic events expected; trading updates from Telenor, SKF, Burberry, Telia, Danske Bank

Opening Call:

European stock futures traded higher early Friday. Asian stock benchmarks were mostly higher; the dollar inched lower; Treasury yields were down; while oil futures and gold weakened.

Equities:

Stock futures were up, as investors continue to look to corporate earnings for cues. Upbeat earnings from some major U.S. companies and strong U.S. retail sales data have lifted optimism that markets can weather tariffs and shifting trade policy.

"Relative to expectations around Liberation Day, tariffs may have a more idiosyncratic impact, depending on the country and sector, given their piecemeal application, delayed starts and potential deals," said Morgan Stanley investment strategist Monica Guerra.

Forex:

The U.S. dollar shuffled between gains and losses amid mixed signals. On the one hand, stronger-than-expected U.S. economic data released overnight could dent rate-cut expectations, thereby supporting the greenback.

However, "USD remains vulnerable to the downside if concerns about U.S. policymaking further undermine investor confidence in USD assets," CBA's Carol Kong said. The dollar's weakness earlier this week due to worries over Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's possible dismissal by President Trump is a case in point, she said.

Bonds:

Treasury yields are holding at levels near key numbers, including 4.5% for the 10-year, and 5% for the 30-year, suggesting that tariffs are only having a mild impact on asset prices, strategists said. But that could change if inflation sets in more durably.

"The Fed will try to see this as transitory," said John Briggs, head of U.S. rates strategy at Natixis Corporate and Investment Banking.

Fed Gov. Christopher Waller called for the central bank to cut interest rates at its July meeting, breaking ranks with Powell. "The risks to the economy are weighted toward cutting sooner rather than later," Waller said, as President Trump intensifies pressure on the Fed to lower borrowing costs.

Energy:

Oil futures consolidated amid mixed signals. On the demand side, economic resilience in the U.S. and China has provided some support, Tickmill's Joseph Dahrieh said.

However, this has been tempered by large increases in U.S. gasoline and diesel stockpiles, casting doubt on the strength of fuel demand as the peak summer travel season progresses, he said.

Metals:

Gold edged higher on possible position adjustments amid a lack of fresh catalysts. However, price gains may be curbed by overnight U.S. data, which showed economic resilience that could damp Fed rate-cut expectations.

-

Iron ore strengthened, buoyed by investors' optimism about improving steel mill margins amid China's efforts to remove overcapacity, and prospects for fresh stimulus for the property sector, ANZ Research said.

The policy that will have the best chance of stabilizing the Chinese property sector is the government buying up excess housing inventory, which can improve the developers' credit health and ease their financial stress, CBA's Vivek Dhar said.

TODAY'S TOP HEADLINES

The Global Risks That Come With the Loss of an Independent Fed

President Trump's threat to attempt to fire Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell has raised a pressing but potentially unanswerable question: What would the global economy and financial markets look like without an independent U.S. central bank?

Shielded from White House interference, the independent Fed has increasingly served as an anchor for U.S. and global markets, stepping in to steady the ship during the 2008-09 financial crisis, the Covid pandemic and other shocks of recent years. Economists credit the central bank's ability to help keep things stable in large part to its power to make moves it deems necessary, regardless of politics.

Bonds Breathe a Sign of Relief at Mild Tariff Data. What Could Change That.

A whiff of tariff inflation showed up in economic data this week, providing the first clues of how tariff policy might impact prices and the economy.

Signals from the bond market suggest tariffs are only having a mild impact on asset prices, strategists say. But that could change if inflation sets in more durably.

German Startup Wants to Regrow Europe's 'Spine' With AI Fighter Pilots, Drone Walls

MUNICH-Inside an unassuming redbrick building tucked beside railroad tracks in the Bavarian capital, engineers behind doors marked "maximum security" toil on drone prototypes, flight simulators and lines of code they believe will shape the next era of warfare.

Helsing, a 4-year-old artificial-intelligence defense startup, has quietly emerged as one of Europe's fastest-growing companies-and a poster child for a continent urgently re-engineering its defense in an era of fracturing alliances.

BHP Flags Higher Costs, Delays on Potash Project; May Sell Nickel Arm

Mining giant BHP Group flagged rising costs and delays at a giant potash project in Canada and said it may sell its Australian nickel operations, as it reported record annual iron ore and copper production.

Australia-based BHP, the world's biggest miner by market value, has been reshaping its business to bet on an accelerating energy transition and global population growth via investments in copper and potash, a fertilizer ingredient. The miner currently relies on the steel ingredient iron ore for more than half its earnings.

Netflix Raises Revenue, Margin Forecasts After Strong Second Quarter

Netflix raised its revenue and operating margin forecasts for the year, continuing a strong run that has propelled its shares to new highs.

The streaming service said member additions, price increases and its growing ad business drove revenue in the second quarter.

Boeing to Sell Up to 18 Dreamliner Jets to Gulf Air

Boeing plans to sell up to 18 of its 787 Dreamliner jets to Gulf Air.

The jet maker said Thursday it has agreed to sell 12 of the jets, with options for six more.

Write to singaporeeditors@dowjones.com

Expected Major Events for Friday

06:00/GER: Jun PPI

07:00/SPN: May Trade Balance

07:30/POR: May Balance of Payments

08:00/EU: May Euro area balance of payments

08:30/UK: Jun Monthly Insolvency statistics

09:00/EU: May Construction output

09:00/ITA: May Balance of Payments

All times in GMT. Powered by Onclusive and Dow Jones.

Write to us at newsletters@dowjones.com

We offer an enhanced version of this briefing that is optimized for viewing on mobile devices and sent directly to your email inbox. If you would like to sign up, please go to https://newsplus.wsj.com/subscriptions.

This article is a text version of a Wall Street Journal newsletter published earlier today.

data.title: China June aluminium imports jump 24.1% versus last year
data.description:
China's imports of unwrought aluminium and products jumped 24.1% year-on-year in June, customs data showed on Friday.

The world's top consumer of the light metal imported 300,000 metric tons of unwrought aluminium and products last month, data from the General Administration of Customs showed.

The data includes primary metal and unwrought, alloyed aluminium.

In the first half of 2025, China imported a total of 1.98 million tons of unwrought aluminium and products, down 3.2% from the corresponding 2024 period.

Imports of bauxite, a key raw material for aluminium, jumped 36.2% on the year to 18.12 million tons in June, driven by higher domestic output of aluminium.

Imports from January to June totalled 103.25 million tons, up 33.6% year-on-year.

data.title: Mapletree Logistics Trust Completes Divestment of Malaysian Property; Shares Up Nearly 1%
data.description:
Mapletree Logistics Trust completed the divestment of Subang 2 in Malaysia, according to a Thursday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

Shares of the trust were up nearly 1% in recent trading.

Following the divestment, the trust's portfolio stands at 176 properties with total value of assets under management at SG$13.2 billion.

data.title: RBA's Shock On-Hold Rates Call Prompts Soul Searching — Market Talk
data.description: The Reserve Bank of Australia's shock decision to keep interest rates on hold this month has prompted a lot of soul searching among economists. Luci Ellis, chief economist at Westpac, has been open with the way she is processing the surprise. The last thing a central bank wants is to be seen as not independent, including from the markets, she says. Cutting the cash rate in July, rather than the RBA's original preference for a cautious and predictable quarterly pace, coinciding with fresh forecasts, would have looked a bit like the market pricing had forced the cut, she adds. There will be more RBA communication next week for economists to ponder. (james.glynn@wsj.com; @JamesGlynnWSJ)

data.title: Australia's lithium miners gain on higher prices
data.description:
** Shares of Australian lithium miners soars on the back of higher lithium prices

** Commodity prices jump after local officials in China halted production at mining giant Zangge Mining's mine

** Mineral Resources gains as much 6.9% to A$29.660, hitting its highest level since Feb 18

** Liontown Resources rises as much as 10.71% to A$0.930, hitting highest level since Oct 29, 2024

** Pilbara Minerals gains 7.3% to A$1.69

** Broader mining sub-index up about 2%, ASX200 benchmark index rises 1.4%

data.title: Telechoice International Exits Singapore Exchange's Watch-List; Shares Surge 12%
data.description:
Telechoice International received in-principle approval from the Singapore Exchange Securities Trading for its application to exit from the Watch-List, according to a Thursday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

Shares of the company surged 12% in recent trading.

The company was removed from the Watch-List, effective July 18.

data.title: Attendo Kicks Off SEK150 Million Share Buyback
data.description:
Swedish health and care services group Attendo will initiate a share repurchase program worth 150 million kronor on Friday.

The program, set to run until Oct. 23, will be carried out by Skandinaviska Enskilda Banken on Nasdaq Stockholm, according to a Thursday release.

data.title: Arta TechFin to Sell Arta Global Markets Subsidiary for HK$6.1 Million; Shares Gain 7%
data.description:
Arta TechFin has agreed to sell its Arta Global Markets subsidiary for HK$6.1 million, according to a Hong Kong bourse filing Thursday.

The financial firm's shares were up over 7% in morning trade on Friday.

Arta TechFin is selling the business to Lau Fu Wing, a director of Arta Global Markets as well as Arta Global Futures, another Arta TechFin subsidiary.

Arta TechFin said the sale would help streamline its business operations.

data.title: Polycab India shares rise nearly 3% on strong Q1; UBS ups target price
data.description:
Shares of Polycab India rallied nearly 3 percent to Rs 7,065 per share in morning trade on July 18 after Citi Research and UBS dished out bullish calls on the stock following its first quarter earnings.

Net profit for the quarter increased by 50 percent from the year-ago quarter to Rs 600 crore, while revenue from operations for the quarter increased by 25.7 percent to Rs 5,906 crore.

Follow our LIVE blog for all the latest market updates

Polycab's Earnings Before Interest, Tax, Depreciation and Amortisation (EBITDA) increased by 47.1 percent to Rs 858 crore. EBITDA margins for the quarter expanded by over 200 basis points from the year-ago period to 14.5 percent.

Following the development, Citi Research maintained a Buy rating with a target price of Rs 7,700 per share, implying an upside potential of 12 percent from the last close on the NSE. The wires and cables segment continued to lead growth, with the FMEG (fast-moving electrical goods) division also showing strength, posting 18 percent revenue growth and a 283 bps expansion in margin to 2.1 percent. Notably, the FMEG business turned in its second consecutive profitable quarter.

Also read: Trading Plan: Can Nifty 50, Bank Nifty manage to rebound amid rangebound trading?

UBS also reiterated a Buy rating on Polycab, raising its target price to Rs 8,100 per share. The new target forecasts an upside of 18 percent. The brokerage highlighted a robust start to the fiscal year and suggested that both the cables and wires (C&W) and FMEG segments delivered strong performances.

Polycab’s wires and cables segment posted a robust performance, with revenue rising to Rs 5,228 crore in the June quarter, up from Rs 3,995 crore a year ago. The FMEG (fast-moving electrical goods) business also saw healthy growth, with revenue increasing to Rs 454 crore from Rs 385 crore in the same period last year. Meanwhile, revenue from the EPC (engineering, procurement, and construction) segment declined to Rs 347 crore from Rs 427 crore a year ago.

Notably, the EPC division, previously clubbed under the ‘others’ segment, will now be reported separately after meeting the qualitative criteria for distinct disclosure.

At about 9:40 am, shares of the company were trading at Rs 7,050, higher by 2.5 percent from the last close on the NSE. Polycab India shares have risen 14 percent in the last month but still trade 5 percent lower on a year-to-date basis.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: BHP Group Mulls Two-year Extension for Jansen Potash Project's Second Stage in Canada
data.description:
BHP Group (, BHG.JO) said Friday it is considering a two-year extension of the second stage of the Jansen potash project in Saskatchewan, Canada, to fiscal 2031 from fiscal 2029.

The mining giant said the reassessment of its plan for the site is part of the regular review of its capital projects. The extension is also being considered amid the potential for additional potash supply to the market in the medium term.

Additional updates on the timing estimate for the project is expected in the first half of fiscal 2026, while an estimate on optimized capital expenditure is expected in the second half of the same fiscal year.

data.title: Beng Kuang Marine Issues Shares Following Warrant Exercise; Shares Up Over 4%
data.description:
Beng Kuang Marine allotted and issued 52,500 shares pursuant to the exercise of warrants at a price of SG$0.22 per share, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

Shares of the company were up over 4% in recent trading.

Following the issue of these shares, the number of issued shares of the company increased to 207,292,973 shares.

data.title: Sadr Logistics Bags SAR14 Million Steel Pallet Supply Deal in Saudi Arabia
data.description:
Sadr Logistics (SASE:1832) secured a 14 million-Saudi-riyal steel pallet supply contract from the National Center for Palms and Dates, according to a Thursday release.

The logistics company will manufacture and deliver steel pallet storage to the customer's affiliated date packaging factory in Al-Ahsa, Saudi Arabia, for 10 months.

data.title: Malaysia Exports Unexpectedly Fall
data.description:
Exports from Malaysia unexpectedly shrank by 3.5% year-on-year to a four-month low of MYR 126.6 billion in June 2025, missing forecasts of a 5.3% increase and following an upwardly revised 1.1% decline in May.

This marked the second consecutive month of falling exports, driven largely by a 9.3% drop in shipments to China, Malaysia’s top trading partner.

data.title: China commerce minister says he met Nvidia CEO in Beijing
data.description:
China's Commerce Minister Wang Wentao said on Friday he met with Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang in Beijing on Thursday.

Wang said at a press conference that Huang had worked very hard over the past few days during his visit to China, but Wang did not provide any details about what was discussed at their meeting.

Nvidia did not respond immediately to a request for comment.

During his third China visit this year, Huang, the founder and CEO of the world's most valuable company, also met with Ren Hongbin, chairman of China Council for the Promotion of International Trade and the country's Vice Premier He Lifeng.

Chinese officials told Huang they welcomed foreign companies to continue to invest in the country, the Nvidia CEO said at a press conference in Beijing on Wednesday.

At the event, Huang described artificial intelligence models from Chinese firms Deepseek, Alibaba and Tencent as "world class" and said AI was "revolutionising" supply chains.

Huang also said Chinese customers' demand for its H20 AI chip, which was released from U.S. export controls this week, is high but no purchase orders have been fulfilled yet as it awaits U.S. government approval for export licences.

Nvidia has also announced it is developing a new chip for Chinese clients called the RTX Pro GPU, which would be compliant with U.S. export restrictions and designed specifically for smart factories and for robot training purposes.

data.title: Global Commodities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Commodities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0250 GMT - Palm oil rises in Asia's early trade, tracking stronger overnight soybean oil on the Chicago Board of Trade and higher palm olein on the Dalian Commodity Exchange, AmInvestment Bank says in a note. It expects CPO futures to find support at 4,224 ringgit a ton and face resistance at 4,335 ringgit a ton. However, ample inventories in India and China, higher supply and weak demand are raising short-term CPO price pressure concerns, Abdul Hameed, director of sales at Pakistan-based Manzoor Trading says. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives October contract rises 70 ringgit to 4,280 ringgit a ton. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0221 GMT - Iron ore rises in early Asian trade. Investors are optimistic about improving steel mill margins amid China's efforts to remove overcapacity, while prospects for fresh stimulus for the property sector has also improved sentiment, the ANZ Research team says in a note. Market expectations of sector-based stimulus are well founded, and the policy that will have the best chance of stabilizing the sector is the government buying up excess housing inventory, which can improve the credit health of developers and ease their financial stress, Vivek Dhar of Commonwealth Bank of Australia says in a note. The most actively traded September iron-ore contract on the Dalian Commodity Exchange is up 1.3% at CNY792.5 a ton. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0137 GMT - RBC Capital Markets sees BHP's "strong" 4Q operational and cashflow performance as the focus of its latest result. That is giving tailwinds to the stock, which is up 2.3% at A$40.02. RBC highlights a 4Q beat on production and FY26 guidance in line with expectations. One-off disclosures are generally positive, although 4Q pricing is mixed, RBC says. The operational result is partially offset by the Jansen stage one capex increase and delay, the broker says. RBC has a sector perform rating and A$44.00 target on BHP. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

0136 GMT - Higher costs at BHP's Jansen potash project offset a strong operational performance, Jefferies analysts say following the miner's 4Q production results. The analysts say the "blowout at Jansen is the clear negative" and that "the fundamental outlook for potash is very challenging, in our view." BHP's 4Q production results are strong, however, they say. "All things considered, we believe the risk/reward tradeoff in BHP is balanced for now." Jefferies reiterates a hold rating on BHP. The stock is up 2.5% at A$40.10. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

0120 GMT - Palm oil demand could get a further boost if Indonesia proceeds with its B50 biodiesel plan, say CIMB analysts Ivy Ng Lee Fang and Lim Yue Jia. The current B40 policy--which mandates a 40% palm-based biodiesel blend--is expected to boost domestic palm oil consumption by 2 million tons and reduce its exportable surplus. A potential shift to B50 in 2026 could raise domestic palm oil use by 3 million tons, they say. This would help offset pressures from U.S. tariffs and support crude palm oil prices, they add. CIMB maintains its 2025 CPO price forecast at 4,200 ringgit/ton and continues to prefer IOI Corp. and Hap Seng Plantations as the plantation sector's top picks. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

2343 GMT - Gold edges higher in the early Asian session on possible position adjustments amid a lack of fresh catalysts. However, gold's price gains may be curbed by U.S. data released overnight, which showed economic resilience that could dampen Fed rate-cut expectations. June retail sales data came in stronger than expected, two members of Sucden Financial's Research team say in a commentary. Also, labour market data reinforced the strength of domestic demand, the members add, noting initial jobless claims fell to the lowest since mid-April. Spot gold is 0.1% higher at $3,341.45/oz. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

1938 GMT - Most-active lean hog futures on the CME rise 1.5% to $1.0595 a pound. The movement in futures wasn't derailed by lower export sales reported by the USDA this morning. But sales reported by the USDA totaled 17,100 metric tons for the week ended July 10 — which is down 30% from the previous week and down 48% from the prior 4-week average. Mexico was the leading buyer for the week, purchasing 7,700 metric tons for the week. Live cattle futures fell 0.1% for the day, sliding to $2.2365 a pound. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1930 GMT - U.S. natural gas futures end a choppy session near flat as a storage report lands in line with expectations and near-term temperature forecasts remain hot but with little change. The EIA said inventories increased by 46 Bcf to 3,052 Bcf last week, leaving them 178 Bcf above the five-year average and 156 Bcf below the year-earlier level. "Widespread heat in the coming weeks should work to limit the rate of storage injections as we move through the peak summer period," Andy Huenefeld of Pinebrook Enbergy Advisors says in a note.Nymex natural gas settles down 0.3% at $3.542/mmBtu. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1918 GMT - Oil futures rebound after three days of losses, supported by better-than-expected U.S. retail sales and a delayed response to yesterday's report of a 3.9 million barrel U.S. crude stock draw. The crude inventory data was overshadowed by large gasoline and diesel inventory builds. "The next move in the petroleum complex might be diesel generated," says Phil Flynn of the Price Futures Group, noting that even after last week's 4.2 million barrel build, stocks are 21% below the five-year average and at their lowest for the time of year in three decades. Nymex diesel futures rise 3.3% to a nearly one-month high. WTI settles up 1.7% at $67.54 a barrel and Brent gains 1.5% to $69.52. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1803 GMT - Concerns that President Trump may attempt to fire Federal Reserve chairman Jerome Powell have receded, deflating movement in gold and making it 3 out of the past 4 trading sessions that gold finishes lower. "Gold rallied on Wednesday on the back of speculation that President Trump was going to fire Fed chair Powell, but the air came out of that balloon as soon as Trump denied he was going to make his move, though he did confirm for the millionth time that he was not happy with the Fed Chair," says Robert Yawger of Mizuho Securities USA in a note. Movement ahead will have to do with the U.S. dollar turning stronger, says Yawger. Front-month gold closes down 0.4% to $3340.10 per troy ounce. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1749 GMT - President Trump's Truth Social post stating that Coca-Cola has agreed to remove high fructose corn syrup from their soda, in favor of using sugar sourced from sugarcane. But high fructose corn syrup is a big consumer of the corn produced in the U.S. — and the majority of sugar made in the U.S. comes from sugar beets, not sugarcane. The USDA says that sugar beets accounted for 55% to 60% of total sugar production since the early 2000s. But most U.S. farmers don't grow beets for sugar, never mind sugarcane — with sugar crops accounting for less than 1% of cash receipts for U.S. farmers on average. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1656 GMT - Oil futures press higher after a cautious start and look set to snap a three-day losing streak. Prices are supported by economic resilience in key oil consuming countries like the U.S. and China, and a resurgence of Middle East risk premium after drone attacks on oil infrastructure in Iraq's Kurdistan region, Pepperstone's Quasar Elizundia says in a note. "However, the market remains sensitive to trade policy and OPEC+ output decisions." WTI is up 1.6% at $67.46 as barrel and Brent rises 1.2% to $69.37.(anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

data.title: Dow Jones Top Financial Services Headlines at 12 AM ET: Fed Gov. Waller Makes the Case for Cutting Interest Rates in 2 Weeks | House ...
data.description:
Fed Gov. Waller Makes the Case for Cutting Interest Rates in 2 Weeks

The Waller speech comes as President Donald Trump intensifies pressure on the Fed to lower borrowing costs.

----

House Passes Crypto Bills After Trump Rallies Republicans

The bills establish standards for stablecoins, lay out rules for crypto exchanges and ban central-bank digital currencies.

----

Charles Schwab's Growth Strategy Tested by Volatile Markets. Earnings Will Show How It's Faring.

Net new assets is a key figure to watch in the company's earnings report.

----

Coinbase Eyes Regulatory Win as Trump Pushes Crypto-Friendly Legislation

Coinbase stands to benefit from the passage of a bill that would set up oversight of stablecoins.

----

Senator Warren Has a New Target on Wall Street: Private Credit

Sen. Elizabeth Warren sent letters Thursday to agencies including S&P Global Ratings, Moody's Ratings and Fitch Ratings, asking for information about how they score the riskiness of private-credit products.

----

U.S.-Based Wells Fargo Banker Blocked From Leaving China

Chinese authorities have placed an exit ban on a China-born executive after she entered the country recently. The bank has suspended travel to China.

----

Does Trump Have the Authority to Fire Fed Chair Powell?

The president may try to terminate the Fed chair's tenure early. But whether Trump has the ability to do so isn't clear.

----

Travelers Stock Is Rising as the Insurer Smashes Earnings Expectations

The property and casualty insurer reports core earnings of $6.51 a share for the second quarter.

----

U.S. Bancorp Second-Quarter Profit Rises Despite Soft Interest Income

U.S. Bancorp notched a higher profit for the latest quarter, though interest income growth was more sluggish than anticipated.

----

Big Banks Are Spinning Market Chaos Into Gold

Jittery markets are swelling trading revenue and profit, prompting banks to put more money to work on Wall Street.

----

Crypto Bills in Limbo After House Vote Stalls. What's Next.

Crypto prices had recovered but GOP lawmakers were at an impasse on crypto legislation despite President Trump's "crypto week" declaration.

----

Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk

Find insight on Canadian consumer spending, U.S. Bancorp and more in the latest Market Talks covering Financial Services.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Energy Headlines at 12 AM ET: FTC Reopens Consent Orders for Proposed Acquisitions from Exxon Mobil, Chevron | Oil ...
data.description:
FTC Reopens Consent Orders for Proposed Acquisitions from Exxon Mobil, Chevron

The FTC has reopened an order involving Chevron's proposed acquisition of Hess, and another regarding Exxon Mobil's proposed purchase of Pioneer Natural Resources after its initial complaints failed to plead any antitrust law violation.

----

Oil Futures Snap Three-Day Losing Streak

Oil futures rebounded after three days of losses, supported by better-than-expected U.S. retail sales and a delayed response to yesterday's report of a 3.9 million barrel U.S. crude stock draw.

----

U.S. Natural Gas Little Changed As Weather Holds

U.S. natural gas futures ended a choppy session near flat as a storage report landed in line with expectations and near-term temperature forecasts remained hot but with little change.

----

Kinder Morgan Results Boosted by Natural Gas Demand

The Houston-based energy infrastructure company said its net income was driven by increased financial contributions from its natural gas pipelines and terminal business segments.

----

Santos Second-Quarter Output Rises as Takeover Campaign Continues

Santos reported a 1% rise in quarterly oil and natural gas production as it waits on a consortium led by Abu Dhabi's national oil company to firm up a $18.72 billion takeover bid.

----

Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk

Find insight on oil futures, SSE and more in the latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities.

----

U.S. Crude Oil Inventories Post Weekly Draw

U.S. crude oil inventories fell last week amid a rise in exports, reversing some of the large stock builds seen the previous two weeks, according to data.

----

TotalEnergies Expects Hit From Lower Oil, Gas Prices

The energy company joined BP in flagging that weaker prices would weigh on results. Shell said earlier it anticipated a hit from weaker performance in its core integrated gas division.

----

KKR-Backed Sol Systems Lines Up $675 Million in Bank Credit for Solar Projects

The new financing comes two weeks after U.S. tax changes that threaten sector investments.

----

OPEC Expects Progress on Trade, Holds Oil-Demand Outlook Steady

The Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries said it anticipates an easing of global trade tensions in the coming weeks and kept its oil-demand forecast unchanged as it continues to ramp up output.

----

BP Flags Hit From Lower Oil, Gas Prices but Expects Production Rebound

The energy major warned that lower oil and gas sales could drag on its results, though upstream production is expected to rise quarter-on-quarter.

----

Global Oil Supply to Rise Faster Than Expected, IEA Says

Supply is set to rise three times faster than demand this year, the International Energy Agency said, though seasonal factors are tightening the market in the short term.
